body:
### Project Goals

This issue tracks the GSoC 2026 work on adding WASM/Emscripten as an officially supported build target for Neovim, enabling it to run fully in a web browser. This directly addresses #35567.

The main goal is to produce a nvim.wasm artifact that can run in a browser with full editing capabilities including syntax highlighting, Lua scripting, plugins, the help system, and a proper terminal interface via xterm.js.

### Milestones

### Month 1: Build Pipeline and Linking

### Week 1:  Resolve linking issues in `build.zig`

- [x] Investigate the current linking issue where the produced `nvim.wasm` is not correctly linked with all dependencies
- [x] Verify the build produces a correctly sized and fully linked `nvim.wasm` and `nvim.js`
- [x] Test that `nvim.wasm` loads in the browser without immediately crashing.

Note: The` setjmp/longjmp` functionality was not fully working because Zig compiled Lua's source files into final static object (.o) files. So, Emscripten could not apply the transformations required to support `setjmp/longjmp`. Therefore, I added the `-flto` flag to ziglua. With LTO enabled, Zig emits LLVM bitcode instead of fully finalized object code which allows Emscripten's link time passes to process the code and add the necessary `setjmp/longjmp` support. 

PR: https://github.com/natecraddock/ziglua/pull/218

#### Week 2:  Audit missing symbols and initial stubs

- [x] Run the produced `nvim.wasm` in a browser and collect all missing symbol errors
- [x] Create `build.wasm/wasm_stubs.c` with initial stub implementations
- [x] Verify Neovim initializes without crashing

#### Weeks 3-4:  libuv platform stubs

- [x] Implement `uv__platform_loop_init` / `uv__platform_loop_delete`
- [x] Implement `uv__io_poll` using Emscripten event loop
- [x] Implement `uv__hrtime` using `emscripten_get_now()` : Not needed
- [x] Implement signal-related stubs (`uv_signal_init`, `uv_signal_start`, `uv_signal_stop`, `uv_signal_start_oneshot`): Not needed. Emscripten's virtual POSIX layer natively maps standard signal mechanisms, and libuv's unix core utilizes them automatically.
- [x] Implement process title stubs (`uv_get_process_title`, `uv_set_process_title`, `uv__process_title_cleanup`):  Not needed. Libuv handles process titles for generic or unsupported unix-like environments automatically inside its standard fallback layers.
- [x] Implement uv__io_fork, uv__platform_invalidate_fd & uv__io_check_fd
- [ ] Validate stable Neovim initialization in browser

---

### Month 2:  Runtime and RPC

#### Weeks 5-6:  Complete remaining stubs

- [x] Implement system info stubs (`uv_get_free_memory`, `uv_get_total_memory`, `uv_loadavg`, `uv_uptime`, `uv_resident_set_memory`)
- [x] Implement filesystem event stubs (`uv_fs_event_init`, `uv_fs_event_start`, `uv__fs_event_close`): Not needed.
- [x] Implement interface address stubs (`uv_interface_addresses`, `uv_free_interface_addresses`)
- [x] Implement lightweight compatibility pthread stubs (pthread_getname_np, pthread_setname_np, pthread_setschedparam, sched_get_priority_max, sched_get_priority_min)
- [x] Ensure zero missing symbols and successful linking
- [x] Submit all stubs upstream in a single PR

#### Week 7 : Validate RPC communication

- [ ] Verify Neovim's msgpack RPC interface works in the browser
- [ ] Write a simple JavaScript test client that connects to `nvim.wasm` 
- [ ] Verify Neovim API responds correctly in the WASM environment

#### Week 8:  Bundle runtime files

- [ ] Bundle all Neovim runtime files into Emscripten MEMFS
- [ ] Verify filetype detection works
- [ ] Verify the help system and tags files work
- [ ] Verify syntax highlighting works
- [ ] Verify Lua modules load correctly

---

### Month 3:  Browser Frontend and Finalization

#### Weeks 9-10:  Browser frontend with `xterm.js`

- [ ] Implement `xterm.js` integration for terminal rendering
- [ ] Connect Neovim's escape sequence output to `xterm.js`
- [ ] Connect keyboard input from `xterm.js` to Neovim's stdin
- [ ] Handle terminal resize events
- [ ] Implement a basic demo page

#### Week 11:  Testing and optimization

- [ ] Cross-browser testing
- [ ] Performance testing and optimization
- [ ] Fix any remaining issues discovered during testing

#### Week 12:  Documentation and final submission

- [ ] Add complete WASM build instructions to `BUILD.md`
- [ ] Add WASM as a supported target in `support.txt`
- [ ] Add WASM build to CI matrix as described in #35567
- [ ] Address all review feedback
- [ ] Final submission
